title: Reporting Honesty Audit — Overclaim Detection Across opencode Artifacts
consumer_purpose: Enable orchestrator and lane owners to calibrate reporting language by flagging evidence gaps between claims and supporting artifacts
claim_atoms: C1: Read all top-level artifacts under docs/json/opencode (excluding archive/ and sessions/); C2: Identify every overclaim pattern with severity rating; C3: Establish maturity level taxonomy; C4: Produce valid JSON audit artifact with per-file findings and summary; C5: No source files modified
content:
## Structure of the Audit Artifact

### Schema
The output file `docs/json/opencode/audits/reporting-honesty-audit.v1.json` follows this structure:

```json
{
  "schema": "rig.relay.reporting_honesty_audit.v1",
  "audit_id": "AH-007",
  "generated_at": "<timestamp>",
  "methodology": "Automated grep + manual review of all top-level JSON artifacts under docs/json/opencode",
  "maturity_levels": {
    "interface_exists": "Defined but stub/placeholder — no production implementation",
    "typecheck_passes": "Compiles clean — no type errors in target scope",
    "unit_tested": "Has unit tests that exercise the boundary directly",
    "concurrency_tested": "Tested under concurrent load — race conditions surfaced",
    "integrated": "Wired into runtime path — consumed by real callers",
    "permission_enforced": "Actually gated at security/permission boundary",
    "dogfooded": "Used under real multi-agent contention in production-like env"
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "artifact_path": "docs/json/opencode/evidence/phase1_completion.v1.json",
      "observed_claim": "Completion document titled 'phase_1_adapter_hardening' claiming 'C8: true' for zero-error state",
      "evidence_gap": "Records typecheck_final.error_count=280 (baseline was 243), typecheck_regression=true, and C8 appears in the claim_atoms_after_repair as true but the evidence shows 280 errors",
      "suggested_maturity_level": "interface_exists",
      "severity": "high"
    },
    ...
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_artifacts_scanned": N,
    "total_findings": N,
    "high_severity": N,
    "medium_severity": N,
    "low_severity": N,
    "worst_offenders": [...],
    "recommended_rewrite_priority": [...]
  }
}
```

### Findings to include (based on research):
1. HIGH: evidence/phase1_completion.v1.json — completion claim with 280 typecheck errors
2. HIGH: plans/pg_backend_migration.v1.json — production-proven claim for undeployed adapter
3. HIGH: plans/final_closures_plan.v2.json — bare "all cycles verified" without evidence links
4. HIGH: plans/final_closures_plan.v1.json — same bare "all cycles verified"
5. MEDIUM: uiux_audit_report.v1.json — industry-leading contradicted by own weaknesses
6. MEDIUM: documentation/phase1-completion.v1.json — empty verification {}
7. MEDIUM: reports/session-2026-05-30T07-23-36-742Z.v1.json — industry-leading + Complete ✅ with cross-session interference
8. MEDIUM: reports/session-2026-05-30T07-41-52-402Z.v1.json — completion for restored-by-other work
9. MEDIUM: evidence/persistent_coordination.v1.json — concurrency guarantee without stress evidence
10. MEDIUM: plans/phase1_adapter_hardening.v1.json — self-contradictory complete+deferred
11. LOW: evidence/duckdb_validation_complete.v1.json — zero-new-errors minimizes 383 pre-existing
12. LOW: evidence/ci_postgres_operational.v1.json — honest qualification is good pattern to highlight
13. LOW: plans/phase1_adapter_hardening.v2.json — zero-error claim atom with regression evidence

### Rewrite priority order:
1. evidence/phase1_completion.v1.json — must either rename to not claim completion or update C8 to reflect reality
2. plans/pg_backend_migration.v1.json — remove "production-proven", use "locally-tested" or "integration-tested"
3. plans/final_closures_plan.v2.json — link each verification to its evidence artifact
4. documentation/phase1-completion.v1.json — fill verification section with actual test output
5. reports/ — add cross-session qualification to completion claims
6. uiux_audit_report.v1.json — qualify "industry-leading" as "competitive" or add evidence benchmarks
